**Subjective**
Kevin's chief concern today was that he feels pretty good overall but has noticed he's been a little more tired than usual, which he attributed to getting older.

**Chief Complaint**
Kevin's primary reason for visit was his annual physical.

**History of Present Illness**
Kevin has noticed he's been a little more tired than usual, which he attributed to getting older. He also has occasional tension headaches, managed with ibuprofen. He gets a little more winded going up stairs than he used to. He experiences heartburn once or twice a month, usually after spicy food. He has nighttime snacking, chips and crackers. He is generally stable in terms of mood, but gets work-related stress.

**Review of Systems**
Constitutional: No fever, chills, or fatigue noted.
HEENT: No vision, hearing, throat, or nasal symptoms noted.
Cardiovascular: No chest pain, palpitations, or edema noted.
Respiratory: No shortness of breath, cough, or wheezing noted.
Gastrointestinal: No nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, or abdominal pain noted.
Genitourinary: No urinary symptoms, menstrual history, or genital issues noted.
Musculoskeletal: No joint pain, muscle weakness, or mobility issues noted.
Neurological: No headache, dizziness, numbness, or weakness noted.
Psychiatric: No mood, anxiety, or sleep issues noted.
Skin: Some sun damage noted on the forearms and back of the neck.

**Past Medical History**
Kevin has a family history of type 2 diabetes on both his mother's and maternal grandmother's side. His father had a myocardial infarction at sixty-two, and is currently on medications. His mother has type 2 diabetes diagnosed about ten years ago and hypertension. His sister has hypercholesterolemia. His maternal grandmother also had type 2 diabetes.

**Medications**
Kevin is currently not taking any medications.

**Allergies**
No known drug allergies.

**Social History**
Tobacco: Kevin smoked in college for about two years, roughly a pack a week, quit at age twenty-three, hasn't smoked since.
Alcohol: Kevin drinks about four to six drinks a week — a couple beers on the weekend, glass of wine with dinner once or twice a week.
Recreational drugs: Kevin tried marijuana a couple times in college.
Occupation: Kevin works as a senior partner at an accounting firm.
Exercise: Kevin used to play basketball twice a week but that fell apart, now he's walking the dog about twenty minutes most mornings and getting on the Peloton maybe once a week.
Diet: Kevin skips breakfast, just coffee with cream and sugar in the morning. Lunch is usually from the work cafeteria, tries for salads but often ends up with sandwiches. Dinner is home-cooked by his wife, generally healthy — chicken, vegetables, rice. His main issue is nighttime snacking, chips and crackers.
Sleep: Kevin averages six to six and a half hours a night, up late working or watching TV, alarm at six.

**Family History**
Kevin's father had a myocardial infarction at sixty-two, and is currently on medications. His mother has type 2 diabetes diagnosed about ten years ago and hypertension. His sister has hypercholesterolemia. His maternal grandmother also had type 2 diabetes.

**Objective**
**Physical Examination**
Head and neck: Pupils equal, round, and reactive to light, conjunctivae clear. Tympanic membranes normal bilaterally. Nares clear. Oropharynx without lesions, tonsils normal. Thyroid normal size, no nodules palpated. No lymphadenopathy in the neck, axillae, or groin.
Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs, gallops, or rubs. Lungs clear to auscultation bilaterally with good air movement. Abdomen soft, non-tender, non-distended, no organomegaly, normal bowel sounds, no masses.
Skin: Some sun damage noted on the forearms and back of the neck, nothing that looks atypical or worrisome, no concerning moles identified.
Musculoskeletal: Full range of motion in all extremities. Right knee with no swelling, no instability, good range of motion.
Neurological: Alert and oriented, cranial nerves intact, sensation and strength normal.
Genitourinary: Testes normal, no masses, no hernias.

**Vital Signs**
BP: 132/84
HR: 76
Temp: 98.4
RR: 14
SpO2: 98% on room air

**General**
Kevin's overall appearance and condition are generally stable.

**Assessment**
Primary diagnosis or clinical impression: Kevin has borderline stage one hypertension, pre-diabetes, dyslipidemia, and overweight.

**Plan**
1. Lifestyle modification — exercise, weight loss, dietary sodium reduction — and recheck blood pressure in one month.
2. Pre-diabetes — counsel on lifestyle modifications including increased physical activity, dietary changes particularly reducing refined carbohydrates and nighttime snacking, and weight loss. Goal weight loss of fifteen to twenty pounds discussed. Recheck fasting glucose and lipids in three months.
3. Dyslipidemia — elevated LDL at 141, low HDL at 44, elevated triglycerides at 185. Intermediate ASCVD risk at approximately 8 percent with significant family history. Plan is aggressive lifestyle modification first. If LDL does not improve in three to six months, will discuss initiating a statin.
4. Overweight — BMI 29.6. Addressed as part of overall cardiovascular and metabolic risk reduction.
5. Fatigue — most likely related to chronic sleep deprivation, averaging six to six and a half hours per night. Counseled that he needs a minimum of seven hours and that sleep affects weight, blood sugar, blood pressure, and energy levels.
6. Right knee stiffness with exercise — no swelling or instability on exam, likely mechanical. Advised to monitor.
7. Occasional heartburn — once or twice a month with spicy food. No further workup at this time.

**Preventive Care**
Td booster given today, as his last Tdap was in 2015. Flu vaccine given today. COVID booster was done last October, up to date. Hepatitis B series confirmed in records. Shingles and pneumococcal vaccines deferred, not yet indicated at his age.

**Referrals**
Placed a referral to GI for colonoscopy, given he's forty-five and has a family history of colon cancer in his maternal grandfather. Placed a referral to dermatology for a full skin check given sun damage noted on exam and history of significant sun exposure in childhood.

**Assessment and Plan**
Kevin is a forty-five-year-old male presenting for annual physical with the following issues:

One, borderline stage one hypertension at 132/84, up from 120/78 last year. Plan is lifestyle modification — exercise, weight loss, dietary sodium reduction — and recheck blood pressure in one month. Will reassess need for pharmacotherapy at that time.

Two, pre-diabetes — fasting glucose 104, A1C 5.7. Counseled on lifestyle modifications including increased physical activity, dietary changes particularly reducing refined carbohydrates and nighttime snacking, and weight loss. Goal weight loss of fifteen to twenty pounds discussed. Recheck fasting glucose and lipids in three months.

Three, dyslipidemia — elevated LDL at 141, low HDL at 44, elevated triglycerides at 185. Intermediate ASCVD risk at approximately 8 percent with significant family history. Plan is aggressive lifestyle modification first. If LDL does not improve in three to six months, will discuss initiating a statin.

Four, overweight — BMI 29.6. Addressed as part of overall cardiovascular and metabolic risk reduction.

Five, fatigue — most likely related to chronic sleep deprivation, averaging six to six and a half hours per night. Counseled that he needs a minimum of seven hours and that sleep affects weight, blood sugar, blood pressure, and energy levels.

Six, right knee stiffness with exercise — no swelling or instability on exam, likely mechanical. Advised to monitor.

Seven, occasional heartburn — once or twice a month with spicy food. No further workup at this time.
